body.title: CC inbox storage: append-only JSONL at ~/.TheIgors/cc_inbox.jsonl
body.result: JSONL storage layer + 24 tests. append() non-fatal on I/O; read_unread() newest-first by monotonic id; mark_read; TTL purge 30d. 6ca2fec6.
body.description:
Create lab/claudecode/cc_inbox.py implementing the storage layer for CC's notification inbox. Append-only JSONL at ~/.TheIgors/cc_inbox.jsonl. Schema per entry: {ts, kind, ticket_id?, summary, body, urgency, response_expected, read, id}. Writer: append(kind, summary, body, ticket_id=None, urgency='normal', response_expected=False). Reader: read_unread() → list[entry]; mark_read(entry_id) advances per-entry read flag. Cursor cleanup: read_unread drops entries older than 30 days to bound file size.

**Affected files:** new lab/claudecode/cc_inbox.py, tests/test_cc_inbox.py
**Design rules:** docs-live-in-code, test-plan-or-why-not, no-sqlite (JSONL, not sqlite)
**Scope boundary:** IN — append, read_unread, mark_read functions + schema. OUT — producer hook (T-cc-inbox-producer), consumer hook (T-cc-inbox-consumer), email bridge.
**Test plan:** unit tests against tmp_path: append writes JSONL row; read_unread returns only entries with read=False; mark_read flips the flag; TTL purge drops >30d entries.
